perturbation.original_text: Each party to the agreement expressly authorizes each other party to file on its behalf any and all amendments to such statement.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party to the agreement expressly authorizes each other party to file on its behalf amendments to such statement, provided that any amendment affecting the ownership percentage of a party requires prior written consent from that party.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction regarding the authority to file amendments. Originally, each party authorized all amendments. Now, amendments affecting ownership percentage require prior written consent, contradicting the initial blanket authorization. This creates uncertainty about which amendments can be filed unilaterally and which require consent. This will cause further contradiction, as if one party files the amendement without consent, other parties may claim the filing as invalid.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In accordance with Rule 13d-1(k) under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, the undersigned agree to the joint filing on behalf of each of them of a statement on Schedule 13G (including amendments thereto) with respect to the Common Stock of Spring Bank Pharmaceuticals, Inc. and further agree that this agreement be included as an exhibit to such filing. Each party to the agreement expressly authorizes each other party to file on its behalf amendments to such statement, provided that any amendment affecting the ownership percentage of a party requires prior written consent from that party. Each party to this agreement agrees that this joint filing agreement may be signed in counterparts.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by modifying the original blanket authorization for filing amendments. The original text, "Each party to the agreement expressly authorizes each other party to file on its behalf any and all amendments to such statement," allowed any party to file any amendment. The changed text adds a condition: "provided that any amendment affecting the ownership percentage of a party requires prior written consent from that party." This directly conflicts with the original authorization, as it now restricts the ability of a party to unilaterally file amendments related to ownership percentage. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, highlighting that the change itself introduces the contradiction within the document. The change introduces an inconsistency regarding the procedure for filing amendments, leading to a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each party to this agreement agrees that this joint filing agreement may be signed in counterparts.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party to this agreement agrees that this joint filing agreement may be signed in counterparts, except that all parties must sign the initial filing of this agreement, but not subsequent amendments, with electronic signatures.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by stating that the joint filing agreement may be signed in counterparts, but the initial agreement requires signatures from all parties. This contradicts the possibility of counterparts as it implies all signatures must be on the same document for the initial filing. Also, it only approves the use of electronic signatures only for the initial agreements not for further counter parts, adding further complexity and need for written signatures in later agreements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In accordance with Rule 13d-1(k) under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, the undersigned agree to the joint filing on behalf of each of them of a statement on Schedule 13G (including amendments thereto) with respect to the Common Stock of Spring Bank Pharmaceuticals, Inc. and further agree that this agreement be included as an exhibit to such filing. Each party to the agreement expressly authorizes each other party to file on its behalf any and all amendments to such statement. Each party to this agreement agrees that this joint filing agreement may be signed in counterparts, except that all parties must sign the initial filing of this agreement, but not subsequent amendments, with electronic signatures.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original text, stating that while the joint filing agreement may be signed in counterparts, the initial agreement requires signatures from all parties. This introduces a direct contradiction because signing in counterparts inherently implies that not all parties need to sign the same physical document. The requirement for all parties to sign the initial agreement negates the possibility of using counterparts for the initial filing, as stated in the original text, and the contradicted text. Furthermore, the limitation of electronic signatures solely to the initial agreement, as detailed in the changed text, adds complexity and creates a strong contradiction as subsequent filings would require written signatures, contradicting the initial intent of counterpart signatures. This creates an inconsistency in terms of enforceability and the procedure of signing the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: UBS ONCOLOGY IMPACT FUND L.P. ONCOLOGY IMPACT FUND (CAYMAN) MANAGEMENT L.P.
perturbation.changed_text: UBS ONCOLOGY IMPACT FUND L.P. or its designated representative, ONCOLOGY IMPACT FUND (CAYMAN) MANAGEMENT L.P. or its designated representative.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a ambiguity regarding who can execute the agreement, where it was only explicit on the first parties before. The parties now can have a designated representative, but there is no description regarding who those designated representatives would be. This modification contradicts other parts of the contract where specific titles and roles are explicitly named for the original parties, leading to a confusion about who is authorized to sign and bind the entities. For example, the original document references 'MPM Oncology Impact Management GP LLC, By: /s/ Ansbert Gadicke'. Under the new change, it could be someone else and cause in-text contradiction
perturbation.contradicted_text: UBS ONCOLOGY IMPACT FUND L.P. or its designated representative, ONCOLOGY IMPACT FUND (CAYMAN) MANAGEMENT L.P. or its designated representative. By: Oncology Impact Fund (Cayman) Management L.P, By: MPM Oncology Impact Management LP, its General Partner its General Partner By: MPM Oncology Impact Management LP, By: MPM Oncology Impact Management GP LLC, its General Partner its General Partner By: MPM Oncology Impact Management GP LLC, By: /s/ Ansbert Gadicke its General Partner Name: Ansbert Gadicke Title: Managing Member By: /s/ Ansbert Gadicke Name: Ansbert Gadicke Title: Managing Member MPM ONCOLOGY IMPACT MANAGEMENT LP MPM ONCOLOGY IMPACT MANAGEMENT GP LLC By: MPM Oncology Impact Management GP LLC, By: /s/ Ansbert Gadicke its General Partner Name: Ansbert Gadicke Title: Managing Member By: /s/ Ansbert Gadicke Name: Ansbert Gadicke Title: Managing Member
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces the phrase "or its designated representative" for both parties, which creates ambiguity about who is authorized to sign and bind the entities. The contradicted text explicitly names specific roles and individuals (e.g., 'Ansbert Gadicke, Managing Member') as authorized representatives. The addition of 'or its designated representative' without defining who these representatives are or what authority they possess directly contradicts the specific identification of authorized signatories in the contradicted section, leading to uncertainty about who can validly execute the agreement.
